text:
Market today: Weak jobs print (57K vs expectations) signals a softening economy - that's a double-edged sword for tech. Softer data can nudge the Fed toward cuts (good for growth names) but also flags real demand risk. Oil falling on Iran/US progress keeps inflation expectations contained, which helps your chip/tech names stay bid near-term.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest alarm - leveraged 3X decay plus a soft macro print is a brutal combo, and your stop at $200 is close to current price ($217.55); one bad day trips it. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, leveraged, and already down hard - margin amplifies this pain fast, stop at $21.38 is razor-thin from $21.70. SMH (-2.5%) underlying weakness confirms chips are under pressure broadly.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds above $200 on any tech selloff today, and whether SMH finds footing - it leads your whole book.

Bottom line: Your two leveraged losers need active stops respected right now - protect margin before looking at anything new.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is live - if price tags it, exit without hesitation; leveraged decay plus weak jobs data makes holding a loser here dangerous on margin.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is basically here now at $21.70 - consider tightening or cutting early given how new, leveraged, and deep-red this is on margin.
No new buys today - macro softness and chip sector weakness make adding leverage or new tech longs low-conviction; hold and watch SMH for direction first.
